title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
After returning from the Brawnsham Hotel, Carter is attended to by Mrs. Bunscombe, who offers various remedies for his distress, including an herb-stuffed pillow and a medicinal liquid for his bath. Carter declines these offers and waits for Poirot, who is unusually late returning from "The Joyful Cafe." When Poirot finally arrives, he reveals he has been searching for a woman named Jane, fearing she may have been murdered.

Unsettled by the mention of murder, Carter discloses that he witnessed three killings at the Brawnsham Hotel that evening involving two women and one man. He describes the crime scenes as chillingly organized, noting that the three bodies were found lying neatly on the floor with their arms at their sides, palms down, and legs together. Carter further reveals a bizarre detail: each victim had an identical gold cufflink in their mouth, engraved with the letters "PIJ."

Upon hearing this, Poirot becomes agitated and recounts his own encounter with Miss Valentine (also referred to as Miss Jennings or Mrs. Vane). He explains that she had instructed him that if she were found dead, he must tell his policeman friend not to look for the killer and to "not let them talk." While Carter interprets this literally as the victim wanting the killer to go unpunished, Poirot suggests a deeper connection between the instruction "don't let them talk" and the discovery of the gold cufflinks in the victims' mouths.
